programme.title: WhatsApp Obsession: The Murder of Stephanie Hansen
programme.desc: On the 30th of December, 2022, 39-year-old Stephanie Hansen was brutally stabbed to death in her London home - her housemate and friend Sheldon Rodrigues initially appeared grief-stricken, yet behind his facade lay a chilling obsession. In the months before Stephanie's death, Rodrigues planted secret cameras and recording devices in their home and bombarded her with coercive WhatsApp messages - when Steph began a new relationship, his jealousy turned deadly. Through exclusive access to police bodycam footage, WhatsApp records and court evidence, this film reveals how the Metropolitan Police exposed Rodrigues' lies and uncovered the disturbing level of surveillance Steph was subjected to - featuring interviews with Steph's family, friends and the detectives who investigated the case, this powerful documentary unravels a story of obsession, control, and the ultimate betrayal.

programme.sub-title: Insurer Won't Help as Our Home Falls Apart
programme.desc: The team meets the couple who have been stuck in a battle with their insurer for more than three years over fixing damage to their home caused by subsidence. At the advice clinic in Reading, there is expert help for one man who suddenly discovered he had been paying a monthly book subscription that wasn't even his for more than three years. Also on the programme, the renters stuck waiting for faster broadband because their landlords won't approve the upgrade.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy is in Norwich to see a Grade II listing with bags of charm and potential. Martin is in Folkestone to visit a two-bed maisonette taken on by a builder with an interesting hobby. Dion heads to Wigan to see a three-bed terrace that's riddled with damp but has great development potential.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Attenborough and the Mammoth Graveyard
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough joins an archaeological dig uncovering Britain's biggest mammoth discovery in almost 20 years. In 2017, in a gravel quarry near Swindon, two amateur fossil hunters found an extraordinary cache of Ice Age mammoth remains and a stone hand-axe made by a Neanderthal. Professor Ben Garrod joins the team at DigVentures during the excavation as they try to discover why the mammoths were here and how they died.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A family want to raise their children near the sea, so Jules Hudson takes them to view a selection of wonderful homes in the Devon and Dorset countryside. Away from houses, Jules takes to a three-wheeled bicycle.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp hosts as a team of pop stars - Pussycat Doll Kimberly Wyatt, Claire Richards from Steps, Eternal's Kéllé Bryan and Sugababe Amelle Berrabah - take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one, they face the bridge, which has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and 10 lies. They must cross by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guests Lady Leshurr, Philippa Perry, Alan Titchmarsh and Mike Wozniak.

programme.title: Election 26
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg is your guide as the results of this year's council elections in England begin to flood in. Reeta Chakrabarti will crunch the numbers at her giant screen, and Professor Sir John Curtice will be on hand to offer his psephological knowledge and insight. BBC regional political editors will be at the most important counts, and representatives of the main political parties will give their reaction to the emerging political landscape.

programme.title: Mud
programme.desc: While exploring a Mississippi River island, Arkansas boys Ellis and Neckbone encounter Mud, a fugitive who needs their help.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Vale of Evesham - Bridging the Hungry Gap
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild microclimate have long made it one of Britain's most productive growing regions.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: At the halfway stage of the race, the teams discover their next checkpoint lies within Zaamin National Park, Uzbekistan. To reach it, they must navigate the endless horizons of the vast Kazakh steppe.

programme.desc: A preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce, and Brian provides an update on the veg he sowed at the start of the series.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael explores the history of Hokkaido's indigenous Ainu people. At Lake Shikotsu, he enjoys a traditional meal of salmon soup and witnesses a performance of the ancient Dance of the Cranes.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fly-Tippers Get Caught in the Act
programme.desc: Farmers confront criminals, wildfires rage and hidden horrors emerge on a Welsh farm.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The freedivers take on a search at a castle's jetty on Windermere, where five years ago a devastated couple lost their treasured wedding ring. Social media detective Mui gets creative in the hope of tracking down the owner of a phone found in the depths, and our crafter Lucy turns a medley of old bottles and jars pulled out of the lake into something quite magical.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at love and relationships in the deaf community, from a couple married for over 50 years to honest conversations about modern dating and finding connection today.

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive travels 200 miles east of Havana to the historic town of Trinidad, a Unesco world heritage site. Along the way, Clive meets the octogenarian carpenter whose skills have contributed to keeping this 16th-century city standing.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers follow lions, leopards and cheetahs in Botswana's Okavango Delta.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: This programme comes from Mellerstain House in the Scottish Borders, where interesting finds include a valuable majolica dish, a dressing case, and a Lowry doodle.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: There's a photo album of RAF Hendon in the 1930s and a rare picture that brings a surprise in the saleroom at the RAF Museum at Hendon in London.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jordan Banjo, Sian Lloyd, Nick Pickard, and Ellie White take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round, and the scores are tallied across the week.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael's South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the world's largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsung's headquarters.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Rivers
programme.desc: Paul explores the upper reaches of the river Dee, which rises higher than any river in the UK. In the heart of the Cairngorm mountains, he learns about writer and mountain lover Nan Shepherd's passion for the living landscape.

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: A compilation of the wildlife documentary series presented by David Attenborough. Captured in stunning ultra-high definition detail and using a whole range of new camera technology, it brings people closer to animals than ever before.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a QI look at vices and virtues with Eshaan Akbar, Jo Brand, Rhys James and Alan Davies.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the FBI, a quiet Hungarian immigrant landscaper, with no apparent ties to organised crime and no known record, concocts an elaborate extortion plan.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.desc: David Olusoga tells the story of London's Covent Garden Piazza. Helped by experts and eyewitnesses, he conjures up ghosts of the past, market traders, sex workers and activists.

programme.title: Waves
programme.desc: The epic emotional journey of a suburban African American family as they navigate love, forgiveness and coming together in the wake of a tragic loss.
